title: Tackling promoters of marketed tax avoidance
body: "These measures target:   promoters of marketed tax avoidance schemes professionals who market or enable the marketing of tax avoidance schemes   New powers will introduce a:   ban on promoting avoidance arrangements that have no realistic prospect of success power to allow HMRC commissioners to specify further arrangements in regulations which may not be promoted, with penalties and a criminal offence for non-compliance   Instead of applying to the courts, HMRC will now issue :   disclosure of tax avoidance scheme (DOTAS) penalties disclosure of tax avoidance schemes: VAT and other indirect taxes (DASVOIT) penalties   HMRC can also:   issue promoter action notices (PANs) which require businesses providing goods and services to promoters to stop issue anti-avoidance information notices (AAIN) which allow HMRC to effectively investigate promoter organisations and identify the responsible individuals, with penalties and criminal offences for those who are non-compliant publish details of legal professionals promoting schemes in more circumstances   "
